description:
You are the on–call operations engineer investigating ticket “INC123”.
    All raw log material for this ticket is already staged on the host
    at

        /home/user/incidents/raw/INC123/

    Your objective is to prepare an archive for hand-off to the
    development team and to extract the one file they immediately need
    for triage.  The work must leave behind a couple of artefacts whose
    exact pathname, filename and textual layout will be checked by an
    automated test.

    What you have right now
    -----------------------
    1.  Directory  : /home/user/incidents/raw/INC123/
    2.  Files      : app.log, error.log and **debug.tmp**
        (all three are regular text files).
    3.  The target directory **/home/user/archives/**
        has already been created and is writable by your user.
    4.  The target directory **/home/user/incidents/triage/**
        has also been created and is writable by your user.

    The final state that MUST exist
    --------------------------------
    A) A compressed **gzip-compressed tar archive** named

           /home/user/archives/INC123_logs.tar.gz

       • It must contain exactly the two files *app.log* and *error.log*
         from the raw incident directory.
       • The file *debug.tmp* must NOT be present inside the archive.

    B) A textual **manifest** named

           /home/user/archives/INC123_manifest.log

       with the following strict layout (including blank lines and
       divider line) so the grader can `diff` it verbatim:

           File Manifest for INC123_logs.tar.gz
           ====================================
           app.log|90
           error.log|58

       Notes for you:
       • “90” and “58” are the byte-sizes of the individual files.
       • The list must be in ASCII sort order by filename.
       • There is a single UNIX newline (LF, 0x0A) after every line,
         including the last one.

    C) The single file *error.log* extracted out of the archive into

           /home/user/incidents/triage/error.log

       The extracted file must be byte-for-byte identical to the
       original source copy that lived under *raw/INC123/*.

    D) A SHA-256 checksum file named

           /home/user/incidents/triage/INC123_checksum.sha256

       with exactly one line, formatted like so:

           error.log: <sha256-hex-digest>

       • The filename part must literally be “error.log”, followed by
         one colon, one space and then the 64-character lowercase
         hexadecimal digest.
       • A single trailing newline (LF) must be present.

       The grader will recompute the SHA-256 digest of
       */home/user/incidents/triage/error.log* during evaluation and
       compare it to the value you recorded, so it must match.

    Requirements & constraints
    --------------------------
    1.  The archive must be created **without** including any “*.tmp”
        files at any depth.
    2.  Use only standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities that are
        available in a minimal Debian/Ubuntu environment
        (e.g. tar, gzip, sha256sum, awk, sort, cut, etc.).
    3.  Do not hard-code the SHA-256 value; compute it from the
        extracted file.
    4.  Keep the total number of terminal commands you issue in the
        range of **5–10**.
    5.  Verbose console output is fine; it will be ignored by the
        autograder as long as the on-disk artefacts are correct.

    Deliverables summary
    --------------------
    1. /home/user/archives/INC123_logs.tar.gz      (archive)
    2. /home/user/archives/INC123_manifest.log     (manifest file)
    3. /home/user/incidents/triage/error.log       (extracted log)
    4. /home/user/incidents/triage/INC123_checksum.sha256 (checksum file)

    When you are satisfied that all four deliverables match the
    specifications above, you may consider the task complete.
truth:
Initial on-disk data before the agent starts
    -------------------------------------------
    • /home/user/incidents/raw/INC123/app.log  
      Content (exact, 2 lines, LF terminated):
        2023-11-12 10:00:00 INFO Application started\n
        2023-11-12 10:05:00 INFO Application stopped\n

      Byte-size: 90

    • /home/user/incidents/raw/INC123/error.log  
      Content (exact, 1 line, LF terminated):
        2023-11-12 10:03:00 ERROR NullPointerException encountered\n

      Byte-size: 58

    • /home/user/incidents/raw/INC123/debug.tmp  
      Content (exact, 1 line, LF terminated):
        temporary file to be excluded\n

      Byte-size: 30

    • Directories already present:
        /home/user/archives/
        /home/user/incidents/triage/

    Expected artefacts after successful completion
    ----------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/archives/INC123_logs.tar.gz  
       – GNU tar archive, gzip compressed.  
       – Contains exactly these two members with correct byte-sizes:
           ./app.log         (90 bytes)
           ./error.log       (58 bytes)
       – No entry named debug.tmp must be present.

    2. /home/user/archives/INC123_manifest.log  
       Exact 4-line text file (LF terminated after every line):

           File Manifest for INC123_logs.tar.gz\n
           ====================================\n
           app.log|90\n
           error.log|58\n

    3. /home/user/incidents/triage/error.log  
       – Must be identical in size (58 bytes) and content to the
         original *error.log* under raw/INC123/.

    4. /home/user/incidents/triage/INC123_checksum.sha256  
       – Exactly one line:
            error.log: <sha256-hex-digest>\n
       – <sha256-hex-digest> must equal the SHA-256 digest of the file
         in #3.  (The grader will compute this digest at run-time.)

    File-system permissions  
    -----------------------
    • All existing directories are 0755 and owned by “user:user”.
    • All existing files are 0644 and owned by “user:user”.
    The agent runs as the same unprivileged “user”.
